names.scientific_name: Monosodium phosphate (E339i), Disodium phosphate (E339ii), Trisodium phosphate (E339iii)

natural_occurrence.description: Sodium phosphates do not occur naturally in free form. Phosphate compounds are ubiquitous in nature as essential components of bones, DNA, and cellular energy systems, but sodium phosphates used in food are synthetically produced.

manufacturing.description: Produced by neutralizing phosphoric acid with sodium hydroxide or sodium carbonate. The degree of neutralization determines which form is produced: monosodium phosphate (E339i, one sodium atom), disodium phosphate (E339ii, two sodium atoms), or trisodium phosphate (E339iii, three sodium atoms). Each has different pH and functional properties.

other_uses.cosmetics: pH adjuster and buffering agent in personal care products.
other_uses.industrial: Detergents, water treatment, metal cleaning, flame retardants.
other_uses.pharmaceutical: Buffering agent, laxative (some forms), pharmaceutical excipient.
other_uses.household: Cleaning products, TSP used as heavy-duty cleaner and paint prep.

eu.conditions: Quantum satis in many categories; specific limits in others. EFSA 2019 raised concerns about total dietary phosphate intake.

safety_evaluations.jecfa_adi: 0-70 mg/kg bw/day (as phosphorus, for all phosphates)
safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: 40 mg/kg bw/day (as phosphorus, for all phosphates)

regulatory_history.event: EFSA published a comprehensive review expressing concern about excessive dietary phosphate intake from food additives in Western diets, particularly in processed foods. Recommended monitoring and potential reduction strategies.

commonly_found_in: Processed cheese and cheese spreads (emulsifying salt)
Processed meats (ham, sausages - improves moisture retention)
Frozen French fries and potato products
fun_facts: Sodium phosphates are the magic ingredient that makes processed cheese melt smoothly and stay creamy
In processed meats, they help retain moisture and improve texture, making products juicier
Western diets often contain high levels of phosphate additives from processed foods, which has raised health concerns
Different forms (mono-, di-, tri-) have different pH levels and are chosen based on the specific food application
Trisodium phosphate (TSP) is also a powerful household cleaner, but food-grade versions are much purer
